title: Review: gc port-drift detection (ga-9shf)
description:
Review commit 85957381 for ga-9shf.

Scope: drift detector (cmd/gc/cmd_doctor_drift.go), start-time WARN log (writeDoltPortFile + startBeadsLifecycle plumbing), gc rig set-endpoint --self --force guard (cmd/gc/cmd_rig_endpoint.go).

All targeted tests pass (TestDoltDriftCheck*, TestSyncConfiguredDoltPortFilesWarnsOnRigPortFileRewrite, TestValidateRigEndpointOptionsSelf*, TestDoRigSetEndpointSelf*). Pre-existing baseline failures in TestOpenStoreAtForCityExec* and TestControllerQueryRuntimeEnvReturnsNilForNonBD are unrelated to this change.

No behavior change to syncConfiguredDoltPortFiles rewrite semantics.
notes:
review_verdict: pass review_commit: 85957381 review_agent: gascity/reviewer-1 reviewed_at: 2026-04-23 verified: - Build clean: `go build ./...` OK at 85957381 (isolated worktree). - Vet clean: `go vet ./cmd/gc/...` OK. - All 16 targeted tests pass at exactly 85957381 (isolated worktree): TestDoltDriftCheckCleanManagedCityIsOK, TestDoltDriftCheckDetectsLiveRigLocalDolt, TestDoltDriftCheckDetectsStaleRigLocalInfo, TestDoltDriftCheckDetectsPortFileDrift, TestDoltDriftCheckNoRigsIsOK, TestRigLocalDoltPIDFromSQLServerInfoParsesColonFormat, TestRigLocalDoltPIDFromSQLServerInfoMissingFile, TestSyncConfiguredDoltPortFilesWarnsOnRigPortFileRewrite, TestSyncConfiguredDoltPortFilesSilentOnNoChange, TestValidateRigEndpointOptionsSelfRequiresPort, TestValidateRigEndpointOptionsSelfRejectsHost, TestValidateRigEndpointOptionsSelfRejectsUser, TestValidateRigEndpointOptionsForceRequiresSelf, TestValidateRigEndpointOptionsRejectsMultipleModes, TestDoRigSetEndpointSelfManagedCityRequiresForce, TestDoRigSetEndpointSelfWithForceSucceeds. done_when_verification: - [x] gc doctor drift detector covers all three patterns (Case A live, Case B port drift, Case C stale info) - cmd/gc/cmd_doctor_drift.go - [x] gc start WARN on port-file rewrite - writeDoltPortFile + stderr plumbed through startBeadsLifecycle->normalizeCanonicalBdScopeFiles->syncConfiguredDoltPortFiles - [x] gc rig set-endpoint --self requires --force under managed_city; prints one-line WARN on success - cmd/gc/cmd_rig_endpoint.go - [x] Integration tests cover both the detector and the start-time warning - [x] No behavior change to syncConfiguredDoltPortFiles rewrite semantics (only added warn-writer plumbing; defense checks verified by existing unmodified tests) security_review (OWASP Top 10): - A1 Injection: no new surface; port parsed via strconv.Atoi with >0 guard; --self forces 127.0.0.1 and rejects --host. - A3 Sensitive data: ports/PIDs not sensitive. - A5 Access control: --force is an operator-mistake guard, not a security boundary; fine. - A6 Security misconfig: this change REDUCES misconfig risk (silent drift becomes visible). - A8 Deserialization: .dolt/sql-server.info parsed as PID:PORT:UUID with strconv.Atoi + >0 guard; trimmed; safe. - A10 Logging: improved — the whole change makes silent rewrites visible. findings: - severity: info category: ux-message confidence: high file: cmd/gc/cmd_doctor_drift.go:131 summary: Stale-info WARN says "pid 0 not alive" when sql-server.info exists but its PID field is empty or non-numeric. rigLocalDoltPIDFromSQLServerInfo returns (0, true, false) in those branches. User still gets "safe to delete" advice; minor cosmetic polish. - severity: info category: doc-consistency confidence: high file: cmd/gc/cmd_doctor_drift.go:135 summary: FixHint for Case A recommends --inherit or --external but does not advertise the new `gc rig set-endpoint --self --port

--force` path shipped in this same commit. Minor doc polish — the detector does not advertise the sanctioned rig-local path the commit introduced. - severity: info category: race-condition confidence: medium file: cmd/gc/cmd_doctor_drift.go:83 (rigLocalDoltPIDFromSQLServerInfo) summary: pidAlive(pid) only checks that SOME process holds the PID; PIDs are recyclable, so a recycled PID that happens to match an old Dolt's could yield a false "live rig-local Dolt" error. Trade-off is reasonable (rare, and cleanup path is safe). A belt-and-suspenders fix would additionally probe the port from the same sql-server.info line. Not blocking. pre_existing_failures: - TestSyncConfiguredDoltPortFilesSkipsNonBDProviders and TestDoRigSetEndpointRejectsNonBDProvider both fail at 98de0f69 (parent of 85957381) as well as at 85957381, so they are pre-existing and NOT regressions introduced by this commit. They are not mentioned in the builder's notes (which listed TestOpenStoreAtForCityExec* and TestControllerQueryRuntimeEnvReturnsNilForNonBD). Worth a separate bead for the test maintainers but not blocking this review. verdict: PASS — routing to gascity/deployer for release-gate.

title: Review: ga-rhw8 — reconciler outcome=deadline_exceeded masking fix
description:
Review PR for ga-rhw8 / ga-ysse3 fix.

## What changed

- cmd/gc/session_lifecycle_parallel.go:523 outcome switch reordered: startCtx.Err() branches now precede err == nil.
- Nil err promoted to wrapped context error in deadline/canceled branches so result.err != nil downstream (required by commitStartResultTraced at :640 to record failure + clear last_woke_at).
- cmd/gc/session_lifecycle_start_deadline_test.go added (investigator authored; builder copied + committed with fix).

## Review checklist

- Switch ordering matches spec exactly (deadline, canceled, err==nil).
- Err-promotion for nil+deadline/canceled routes correctly through commitStartResultTraced.
- No regressions in 'go test ./cmd/gc -run "TestReconcile|TestCommitStart|TestStartPreparedStart|TestExecutePreparedStartWave|TestSessionLifecycle|TestCandidate" -count=1'.
- 'go vet ./cmd/gc' clean.

## Quality gates run by builder

- [x] Reproducing test fails pre-fix, passes post-fix.
- [x] Spec's full test suite passes.
- [x] go vet clean.

## Commit

7e71fd4c on branch gc-builder-1-01561d4fb9ea.

## Scope discipline

- Single file edit + one new test file.
- No city_runtime.go / controller.go touches (ga-yl93o separate).
- No startup_timeout lowering, no Provider refactors.

value: bd create --deps 'blocked-by:X' stores a dep of TYPE blocked-by which bd ready IGNORES as a blocker. Only deps of TYPE blocks (from 'bd dep add CHILD PARENT' or --deps 'blocks:X' where the new bead is the blocker) are respected by bd ready. When child-blocked-by-parent relationships are needed for readiness gating, always use 'bd dep add <dependent> <prerequisite>' after create, or use --deps 'blocks:<new-dependent-id>' at create time on the prerequisite.

Reviewer pass handoff: when passing a bead to gemini-reviewer, REMOVE the needs-review label in addition to adding needs-gemini-review. The role spec shows only 'add-label needs-gemini-review' but leaving needs-review attached keeps the bead stuck on the first-pass reviewer's hook. Pattern confirmed across 9+ passed beads (all had only needs-gemini-review, none retained needs-review).
